methodology_note: Each register entry specifies: candidate ID, current probability estimate, a specific observable falsification test with a check date, a confidence interval, the verification method (LLM-executable via web search / arXiv API), and the latest check result if available.

candidates.name: FCI braiding achieves fault-tolerant logical qubit above cryogenic temperatures
candidates.falsification_test: By 2028-12-31, no fractional Chern insulator (FCI) device demonstrates a logical qubit with T₂* > 1 millisecond at operating temperature > 1 Kelvin in a peer-reviewed publication.
candidates.verification_method: arXiv API search: query='fractional Chern insulator' AND 'coherence' AND ('millikelvin' OR 'kelvin') → extract maximum reported T₂* and operating temperature from top-20 results
candidates.null_hypothesis: FCI coherence times remain below 1ms at all temperatures above 1K — the thermal protection thesis is unproven.
candidates.surprising_result: An FCI device reports T₂* > 1ms at T > 1K — this would be strong positive evidence for the FCI thesis.
candidates.check_result: PRELIMINARY_HOLDS: arXiv search for 'fractional Chern insulator' AND 'coherence' AND 'temperature' returned 2 papers (Spin-Polaron Mediated Superconductivity in Doped Chern Antiferromagnets; Topologically enabled superconductivity in rhombohedral graphene), neither addressing FCI qubit coherence at temperatures above millikelvin. Zero experimental FCI coherence data at T > 1K. Null hypothesis currently holds.
candidates.calibration_note: This candidate was WOUNDED in the evidence synthesis: the FCI thesis was found refuted by its own source paper (ultrametric threshold 55× worse than surface codes). Probability already revised downward from initial estimate.

candidates.name: Gate-based quantum computing hits thermal runaway wall at 10k+ qubits
candidates.falsification_test: By 2030-12-31, at least one gate-based quantum processor scales to >10,000 physical qubits with two-qubit gate fidelity >99.9% and maintains operational stability at milliKelvin temperatures for >1 hour without thermal runaway shutdown.
candidates.verification_method: Monitor Google Quantum AI / IBM Quantum / Quantinuum published roadmaps and peer-reviewed results. Check: announced qubit count, gate fidelity, and any reports of thermal management breakthroughs.
candidates.null_hypothesis: No gate-based processor exceeds 5,000 qubits with >99.9% fidelity by 2030 — the thermal wall holds.
candidates.surprising_result: A gate-based processor scales past 10k qubits without thermal issues — this directly falsifies Era 1's core claim that active QEC is a thermodynamic dead end.
candidates.calibration_note: This is the Continuity Baseline candidate. Historical roadmap data: Google Willow (2024) = 105 qubits; IBM Osprey (2023) = 1,121 qubits. Scaling trajectory: ~10× per 3-4 years → 10k qubits by ~2032-2035. Thermal wall claim requires a deviation from this trajectory.

candidates.name: Bio-spintronic √SWAP radical-pair gate demonstrated at room temperature
candidates.falsification_test: By 2030-12-31, no published experiment demonstrates spin coherence retention > 1 millisecond in a synthetic biomimetic channel at 310K with controllable √SWAP gate operation.
candidates.verification_method: arXiv + PubMed search: query=('radical pair' OR 'spin coherence') AND ('room temperature' OR '310K') AND ('gate' OR '√SWAP' OR 'controllable') → extract reported coherence times and gate fidelities from top-20 results.
candidates.null_hypothesis: Room-temperature spin coherence in synthetic biological channels remains below 1ms and no controllable gate is demonstrated — the bio-spintronics path is speculative.
candidates.surprising_result: Any paper demonstrating >1ms coherence at 310K with controllable spin manipulation — this would be the first experimental validation of Era 2.
candidates.calibration_note: Current state: radical-pair mechanism is experimentally confirmed in cryptochrome (magnetoreception), but coherence times in synthetic channels are unmeasured. No √SWAP gate has been demonstrated in a biological substrate.

candidates.name: Superdeterminism CMB anomalies pass statistical significance threshold
candidates.falsification_test: By 2032-12-31, no peer-reviewed CMB analysis attributes anomalous large-scale correlations (e.g., hemispherical power asymmetry, low-ℓ anomaly) to superdeterministic cosmic initial conditions with p < 0.01 after look-elsewhere correction.
candidates.verification_method: arXiv search: query='superdeterminism' AND 'CMB' AND ('anomaly' OR 'anisotropy') → classify papers as 'supporting QNFO thesis' or 'neutral/contradicting'. Count supporting papers with p < 0.01.
candidates.null_hypothesis: Superdeterminism remains a fringe interpretation with zero statistically significant CMB predictions — the hydrodynamic foundations thesis has no observational anchor.
candidates.surprising_result: A peer-reviewed paper shows CMB anomalies are predicted by superdeterministic initial conditions at p < 0.01 — this would be the first empirical support for Era 3.
candidates.calibration_note: Current status: superdeterminism is theoretically coherent (Hossenfelder, Palmer, 't Hooft) but has produced zero experimentally verified predictions distinct from standard ΛCDM. The 'superdeterminism conspiracy' objection (Bell's theorem loophole) remains unresolved in the physics community.

candidates.name: Standard Model coupling constants derived from π/φ geometric ratios
candidates.falsification_test: By 2035-12-31, no peer-reviewed paper derives any Standard Model coupling constant (α, α_s, sin²θ_W, or Yukawa couplings) from pure π/φ geometric ratios with precision matching experimental values to within 1%.
candidates.verification_method: arXiv + INSPIRE search: query=('standard model' OR 'coupling constant' OR 'fine structure') AND ('golden ratio' OR 'phi' OR 'π' OR 'geometric derivation') → extract claimed derivations and compare precision to PDG experimental values.
candidates.null_hypothesis: No purely geometric derivation reproduces any SM constant to within 1% of experimental precision — the π/φ geometry thesis is numerological, not physical.
candidates.surprising_result: A published derivation reproduces α ≈ 1/137.036 from pure geometry — this would be a genuinely revolutionary result.
candidates.calibration_note: Historical context: Arthur Eddington (1929) attempted to derive α from pure number theory and failed. Multiple subsequent attempts (Wyler, Gilson, etc.) have been published but none achieved consensus acceptance. This field has a high base rate of false positives.

summary_statistics.next_calibration_check: 2026-12-31 (6-month: re-scan arXiv for preliminary results affecting any candidate)

back_testing_cadence.6_month: Re-scan arXiv + Semantic Scholar for new preprints affecting any registered candidate
back_testing_cadence.12_month: Update probability estimates based on new evidence; compute calibration error
back_testing_cadence.24_month: Full review: any probability shift >20%? If yes, re-run Bayesian cascade model
back_testing_cadence.60_month: Classify all candidates with closed test windows as HIT / MISS / INCONCLUSIVE
